what_it_does: A Supabase Deno edge function acting as a thin OAuth-facing MCP gateway: it serves OAuth protected-resource discovery, returns 401 with a WWW-Authenticate pointing at its own discovery when no Bearer token is present, and forwards authenticated /mcp POST requests verbatim to the backend box (remote.py over a Tailscale Funnel), passing the box's response straight back.
observations: Reads SUPABASE_URL (auto-injected) and an overridable COMPANY_BACKEND_URL, defaulting to a Tailscale Funnel host (workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net) with trailing slashes stripped.
Handles four path suffixes: /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource (discovery JSON), /healthz (status), /mcp (the forwarder), and a 404 fallback for unknown paths.
Discovery returns resource = this gateway's /mcp URL, authorization_servers = origin/auth/v1, bearer_methods_supported = ['header'].
For /mcp: GET returns 405, non-POST returns 404, missing/invalid Bearer returns 401 with WWW-Authenticate resource_metadata.
Forwards request body and Authorization header to BACKEND_URL/mcp; on fetch failure returns 502 with the error message.
If the upstream box returns 401, the gateway rewrites WWW-Authenticate to re-point at its own discovery URL so the app re-auths against the correct resource.
Comments explicitly state the gateway does NO tool logic, holds NO tool list, makes NO access decision; the box re-validates the JWT and owns the tool registry and posture filter.
Comments note deletion of clients.allowed_tools (15-tool column) and the gateway's own dispatch(), to avoid duplicated menu/auth.
Serves via Deno.serve with configurable PORT (default 8000) and HOST (default 127.0.0.1).
standouts: Explicit single-brain architecture: the gateway is deliberately a forwarder while the box (remote.py) owns auth, the tool registry, and the identity-to-posture access filter.
401 re-pointing on upstream 401 keeps the app's OAuth bound to the stable cloud resource even though real validation happens at the box.
conventions: Uses small helper functions (json, publicOrigin) and a single handler dispatched by pathname suffix.
Treats the backend Funnel URL as a public address, not a secret, so no extra gateway secrets are needed.
Documents a deliberate de-duplication: removing duplicated tool list and auth decision from the gateway layer.
concerns: Routing matches on url.pathname.endsWith(...), which could match unintended longer paths that happen to end with these suffixes.
publicOrigin() returns SUPABASE_URL directly with no fallback validation; if SUPABASE_URL is empty the discovery and 401 URLs would be malformed.
notes: A deliberately brainless OAuth/MCP cloud door: serves discovery, demands a Bearer token, and forwards everything to the box which does the real auth, tool listing, and dispatch.
questions: What posture/identity rules does the box apply (the comment mentions operator gets all 66 tools, other valid users get a posture-safe subset) — those rules are not in this file?
Is the trailing-suffix path matching ever a risk given Supabase's function URL prefixing?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header describes a thin OAuth-facing forwarder that holds no tool logic or auth decision, and the code implements exactly discovery, 401-bootstrap, and verbatim forwarding.
apparent_themes: One brain / single source of truth for auth and tools
Stable cloud OAuth resource decoupled from the real backend
Eliminating duplicated authorization decisions and tool menus
intention_signals: Tries to satisfy the OAuth requirement that the resource live at a stable cloud URL while keeping all real logic (JWT validation, tool registry, posture filtering, dispatch) in one place on the box, so the cloud layer can never drift into a second, conflicting authority.
novelty: The intentional reduction of an edge function to a pure forwarder, with comments documenting what was deleted to prevent duplication, is unusual and architecturally explicit.
contribution: Contributes the stable cloud OAuth resource endpoint and the discovery + 401-bootstrap + verbatim-forwarding behavior that bridges the app's OAuth to the box backend without holding any auth or tool state.
summary_for_embedding: A Supabase Deno edge function that serves OAuth protected-resource discovery and forwards authenticated MCP POST requests verbatim to a Tailscale-Funnel backend box, holding no tool logic or auth decision itself.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a stable cloud OAuth resource door for the Claude Design connector while keeping all real authentication, tool registry, and access decisions on the single backend box.
